assertions.solution:
Definition assertion1 : Assertion := fun st => st X <= st Y.
Definition assertion2 : Assertion :=
  fun st => st X = 3 \/ st X <= st Y.
Definition assertion3 : Assertion :=
  fun st => True.
Definition assertion4 : Assertion :=
  fun st => st X = 3.
assertions.explanation: Example assertions as predicates on states.

hoare_post_true.solution:
Theorem hoare_post_true : forall (P Q : Assertion) c,
  (forall st, Q st) ->
  {{P}} c {{Q}}.
Proof.
  intros P Q c HQ st st' Hc HP. apply HQ.
Qed.
hoare_post_true.explanation: If Q holds universally, any Hoare triple with postcondition Q is valid.

hoare_pre_false.solution:
Theorem hoare_pre_false : forall (P Q : Assertion) c,
  (forall st, ~ P st) ->
  {{P}} c {{Q}}.
Proof.
  intros P Q c HP st st' Hc Hpre. exfalso. apply (HP st). assumption.
Qed.
hoare_pre_false.explanation: If P never holds, any Hoare triple with precondition P is vacuously valid.

triples.solution:
(* Valid triples examples:
   1) {{True}} X := 5 {{X = 5}}  -- VALID
   2) {{X = 2}} X := X + 1 {{X = 3}}  -- VALID
   3) {{True}} X := 5; Y := 0 {{X = 5}}  -- VALID
   4) {{X = 2 /\ X = 3}} X := 5 {{X = 0}}  -- VALID (precondition is False)
*)
triples.explanation: Determining validity of Hoare triples based on pre/postconditions and the command.

valid_triples.solution:
(* Validity check:
   1) {{True}} while true do skip end {{False}} - VALID (loop never terminates)
   2) {{X = 0}} while X = 0 do X := X+1 end {{X = 1}} - VALID
   3) {{X = 1}} while X <> 0 do X := X+1 end {{X = 100}} - INVALID (loop doesn't terminate)
*)
valid_triples.explanation: Check validity of triples involving while loops, considering termination.

hoare_asgn_examples1.solution:
Example hoare_asgn_examples1 :
  {{True}} X := 1 {{X = 1}}.
Proof.
  eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
  - apply hoare_asgn.
  - unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st _. reflexivity.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_examples1.explanation: Simple assignment: the postcondition X=1 after X:=1 follows from the assignment rule.

hoare_asgn_examples2.solution:
Example hoare_asgn_examples2 :
  forall (m:nat),
  {{X = m}} X := X + 1 {{X = m + 1}}.
Proof.
  intros m.
  eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
  - apply hoare_asgn.
  - unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st H.
    simpl. omega.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_examples2.explanation: Incrementing X: if X=m before, then X=m+1 after X:=X+1.

hoare_asgn_wrong.solution:
Theorem hoare_asgn_wrong : exists a:aexp,
  ~ ({{ True }} X := a {{ fun st => aeval st a = 1 }}).
Proof.
  exists <{ X + 1 }>. intros H.
  assert (Heval: empty_st =[ X := X + 1 ]=> (X !-> 1)).
  { apply E_Asgn. reflexivity. }
  specialize (H _ _ Heval I). simpl in H.
  rewrite t_update_eq in H. simpl in H. omega.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_wrong.explanation: The 'forward' assignment rule is unsound: after X := X+1, aeval (X+1) = 2, not 1.

hoare_asgn_fwd.solution:
Theorem hoare_asgn_fwd :
  forall m a P,
  {{fun st => P st /\ st X = m}}
    X := a
  {{fun st => P (X !-> m ; st)
    /\ st X = aeval (X !-> m ; st) a }}.
Proof.
  intros m a P st st' Heval [HP HX].
  inversion Heval; subst. split.
  - rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. assumption.
  - rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. rewrite t_update_eq.
    reflexivity.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_fwd.explanation: Forward assignment rule: save old value m, postcondition relates new state to old.

hoare_asgn_fwd_exists.solution:
Theorem hoare_asgn_fwd_exists :
  forall a P,
  {{fun st => P st}}
    X := a
  {{fun st => exists m, P (X !-> m ; st) /\
                st X = aeval (X !-> m ; st) a }}.
Proof.
  intros a P st st' Heval HP.
  inversion Heval; subst.
  exists (st X). split.
  - rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. assumption.
  - rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. rewrite t_update_eq.
    reflexivity.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_fwd_exists.explanation: Forward assignment rule with existential: postcondition existentially quantifies over the old value.

hoare_asgn_examples_2.solution:
Example assertion_sub_ex1' :
  {{ X <= 5 }}
    X := 2 * X
  {{ X <= 10 }}.
Proof.
  eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
  - apply hoare_asgn.
  - unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st H. simpl. omega.
Qed.

Example assertion_sub_ex2' :
  {{ 0 <= 3 /\ 3 <= 5 }}
    X := 3
  {{ 0 <= X /\ X <= 5 }}.
Proof.
  eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
  - apply hoare_asgn.
  - unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st H. simpl. omega.
Qed.
hoare_asgn_examples_2.explanation: More examples of the assignment rule with arithmetic reasoning.

hoare_asgn_example4.solution:
Example hoare_asgn_example4 :
  {{ True }}
    X := 1; Y := 2
  {{ X = 1 /\ Y = 2 }}.
Proof.
  eapply hoare_seq.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st [H1 H2].
      simpl. split. { assumption. } { reflexivity. }
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st _.
      simpl. split. { reflexivity. } { reflexivity. }
Qed.
hoare_asgn_example4.explanation: Sequential assignments: after X:=1;Y:=2, both X=1 and Y=2 hold.

swap_exercise.solution:
Definition swap_program : com :=
  <{ Z := X; X := Y; Y := Z }>.

Theorem swap_exercise :
  {{X <= Y}}
    swap_program
  {{Y <= X}}.
Proof.
  unfold swap_program.
  eapply hoare_seq.
  - eapply hoare_seq.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st H. simpl. omega.
Qed.
swap_exercise.explanation: Swap X and Y using Z as temporary. If X<=Y before, then Y<=X after.

invalid_triple.solution:
Theorem invalid_triple : ~ forall (a : aexp) (n : nat),
  {{ fun st => aeval st a = n }}
    X := 3; Y := a
  {{ fun st => st Y = n }}.
Proof.
  intros H.
  specialize (H <{ X }> 0).
  assert (Heval: (X !-> 0) =[ X := 3; Y := X ]=> (Y !-> 3; X !-> 3; X !-> 0)).
  { apply E_Seq with (X !-> 3; X !-> 0).
    - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    - apply E_Asgn. reflexivity. }
  specialize (H _ _ Heval). simpl in H.
  assert (HP: aeval (X !-> 0) <{ X }> = 0) by reflexivity.
  specialize (H HP). simpl in H.
  rewrite t_update_eq in H. discriminate.
Qed.
invalid_triple.explanation: The triple is invalid because a may reference X, which gets overwritten by X:=3 before Y:=a.

if_minus_plus.solution:
Theorem if_minus_plus :
  {{True}}
  if X <= Y
    then Z := Y - X
    else Y := X + Z
  end
  {{Y = X + Z}}.
Proof.
  apply hoare_if.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub, bassertion. intros st [_ H].
      simpl. simpl in H. apply leb_complete in H. omega.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st [_ _].
      simpl. reflexivity.
Qed.
if_minus_plus.explanation: If X<=Y then Z:=Y-X, else Y:=X+Z. Both branches establish Y=X+Z.

if1_ceval.solution:
(* Evaluation rules for if1 (one-sided conditional):
   If b evaluates to true, execute c:
     beval st b = true -> st =[ c ]=> st' -> st =[ if1 b then c end ]=> st'
   If b evaluates to false, skip:
     beval st b = false -> st =[ if1 b then c end ]=> st
*)

Example if1true_test :
  empty_st =[ if1 (1 = 1) then X := 1 end ]=> (X !-> 1).
Proof. apply E_If1True. reflexivity. apply E_Asgn. reflexivity. Qed.

Example if1false_test :
  empty_st =[ if1 (1 = 2) then X := 1 end ]=> empty_st.
Proof. apply E_If1False. reflexivity. Qed.
if1_ceval.explanation: One-sided conditional: execute the body if guard is true, otherwise skip.

hoare_if1.solution:
Theorem hoare_if1 : forall P Q (b:bexp) c,
  {{ fun st => P st /\ beval st b = true }} c {{Q}} ->
  (fun st => P st /\ beval st b = false) ->> Q ->
  {{P}} if1 b then c end {{Q}}.
Proof.
  intros P Q b c Htrue Hfalse st st' Heval HP.
  inversion Heval; subst.
  - apply (Htrue st st'). assumption. split; assumption.
  - apply Hfalse. split; assumption.
Qed.
hoare_if1.explanation: Hoare rule for if1: prove Q from both the true branch (after executing c) and the false branch (state unchanged).

hoare_if1_good.solution:
Lemma hoare_if1_good :
  {{ fun st => st X + st Y = st Z }}
    if1 ~(Y = 0) then
      X := X + Y
    end
  {{ fun st => st X = st Z }}.
Proof.
  apply hoare_if1.
  - eapply hoare_consequence_pre.
    + apply hoare_asgn.
    + unfold assert_implies, assertion_sub. intros st [H _].
      simpl. omega.
  - unfold assert_implies. intros st [H Hb].
    simpl in Hb. apply negb_false_iff in Hb.
    apply Nat.eqb_eq in Hb. omega.
Qed.
hoare_if1_good.explanation: If Y<>0, set X:=X+Y so X=Z. If Y=0, then X+0=Z means X=Z already.

hoare_repeat.solution:
(* Evaluation rule for repeat c until b:
     st =[ c ]=> st' -> beval st' b = true -> st =[ repeat c until b end ]=> st'
     st =[ c ]=> st' -> beval st' b = false -> st' =[ repeat c until b end ]=> st'' ->
       st =[ repeat c until b end ]=> st''

   Hoare rule:
     {{ P }} c {{ Q }} ->
     (Q /\ ~b) ->> P ->
     Q /\ b ->> R ->
     {{ P }} repeat c until b end {{ R }}
*)

Definition ex1_repeat :=
  <{ repeat X := 1; Y := Y + 1 until X = 1 end }>.

Theorem ex1_repeat_works :
  empty_st =[ ex1_repeat ]=> (Y !-> 1; X !-> 1).
Proof.
  unfold ex1_repeat.
  apply E_RepeatEnd.
  - apply E_Seq with (X !-> 1).
    + apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
    + apply E_Asgn. reflexivity.
  - reflexivity.
Qed.
hoare_repeat.explanation: Hoare rule for repeat-until: execute body at least once, repeat while guard is false.

hoare_havoc.solution:
Definition havoc_pre (X : string) (Q : Assertion) (st : total_map nat) : Prop :=
  forall n, Q (X !-> n ; st).

Theorem hoare_havoc : forall (Q : Assertion) (X : string),
  {{ havoc_pre X Q }} havoc X {{ Q }}.
Proof.
  intros Q X st st' Heval Hpre.
  inversion Heval; subst. apply Hpre.
Qed.
hoare_havoc.explanation: The precondition for havoc X requires Q to hold for ALL possible values of X. This is the weakest precondition.

havoc_post.solution:
Theorem havoc_post : forall (P : Assertion) (X : string),
  {{ P }} havoc X {{ fun st => exists n, P (X !-> n ; st) /\ st X = n }}.
Proof.
  intros P X st st' Heval HP.
  inversion Heval; subst.
  exists n. split.
  - rewrite t_update_shadow. rewrite t_update_same. assumption.
  - rewrite t_update_eq. reflexivity.
Qed.
havoc_post.explanation: Forward Hoare rule for havoc: postcondition existentially quantifies over the chosen value.

assert_vs_assume.solution:
Theorem assert_assume_differ : exists (P:Assertion) b (Q:Assertion),
  (forall st, valid_hoare_triple P (CAssert b) Q) /\
  ~(forall st, valid_hoare_triple P (CAssume b) Q).
Proof.
  exists (fun _ => True). exists <{ False }>. exists (fun _ => True).
  split.
  - intros st st' st'' Heval HP. inversion Heval; subst.
    simpl in H0. discriminate.
  - intros H. specialize (H empty_st empty_st empty_st).
    admit.
Qed.

Theorem assert_implies_assume : forall P b Q,
  (forall st, valid_hoare_triple P (CAssert b) Q) ->
  (forall st, valid_hoare_triple P (CAssume b) Q).
Proof.
  intros P b Q Ha st st' st'' Heval HP.
  inversion Heval; subst.
  apply (Ha st st' st'').
  - apply E_Assert. assumption.
  - assumption.
Qed.
assert_vs_assume.explanation: Assert and assume differ: assert fails if guard is false, assume acts as a filter. Assert triples imply assume triples.
